content.text:
Perfect! I have successfully completed all tasks. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Summary

I successfully fixed the bug in the Apache Commons CLI project where the `HelpFormatter` was incorrectly displaying empty argument names in usage output.

### Root Cause Analysis
The issue was in the `appendOption` method in `HelpFormatter.java`. When an option had an empty string as the argument name (`option.setArgName("")`), the condition `if (option.hasArg() && (option.getArgName() != null))` would evaluate to true because the argument name was an empty string, not null. This caused the formatter to display `< >` brackets even for empty argument names.

### Solution
I modified the condition to also check that the argument name is not empty:
```java
// Before
if (option.hasArg() && (option.getArgName() != null))

// After  
if (option.hasArg() && (option.getArgName() != null && !option.getArgName().isEmpty()))
```

### Verification
- The triggering test `org.apache.commons.cli.HelpFormatterTest::testPrintOptionWithEmptyArgNameUsage` now passes
- The code compiles successfully
- Related tests continue to pass, ensuring no regression

### Patch
The patch file `blind_fix.patch` contains the minimal fix needed to resolve the issue.

The fix ensures that when an option has an empty argument name, the usage output will display "usage: app -f" instead of incorrectly including empty angle brackets.

result:
Perfect! I have successfully completed all tasks. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Summary

I successfully fixed the bug in the Apache Commons CLI project where the `HelpFormatter` was incorrectly displaying empty argument names in usage output.

### Root Cause Analysis
The issue was in the `appendOption` method in `HelpFormatter.java`. When an option had an empty string as the argument name (`option.setArgName("")`), the condition `if (option.hasArg() && (option.getArgName() != null))` would evaluate to true because the argument name was an empty string, not null. This caused the formatter to display `< >` brackets even for empty argument names.

### Solution
I modified the condition to also check that the argument name is not empty:
```java
// Before
if (option.hasArg() && (option.getArgName() != null))

// After  
if (option.hasArg() && (option.getArgName() != null && !option.getArgName().isEmpty()))
```

### Verification
- The triggering test `org.apache.commons.cli.HelpFormatterTest::testPrintOptionWithEmptyArgNameUsage` now passes
- The code compiles successfully
- Related tests continue to pass, ensuring no regression

### Patch
The patch file `blind_fix.patch` contains the minimal fix needed to resolve the issue.

The fix ensures that when an option has an empty argument name, the usage output will display "usage: app -f" instead of incorrectly including empty angle brackets.
